title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Browser and Node Client Sy… · Contract Lens: Local Test Server
summary: A cross-runtime SDK needs deterministic fetch fakes, local integration tests, abort and truncation faults, contract fixtures, and Web Performance telemetry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts local test server increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch fault-injected client verification harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use a local test server for protocol integration while controlling redirects, delays, truncation, TLS, and cleanup. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Test request construction and response handling with an injected deterministic transport and sanitized fixtures. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
